name: Petit Lenormand
image_credit: Art & card text by Yve Lepkowski (stolen-thyme.com), CC-BY-SA-4.0.
description:
A 34-card Lenormand oracle deck with playful artistic interpretations of the traditional symbols.

The Lenormand system, named after the famous French fortune-teller Marie Anne Lenormand (1772-1843), uses a 36-card deck with straightforward symbolic imagery. Unlike tarot, Lenormand cards are typically read in combinations where meanings blend together.

This version includes some creative variations: Goose (instead of Stork), Rabbits (instead of Mice), and Dove (instead of Lily). Note: This edition omits cards 8 (Coffin) and 9 (Bouquet).

Artwork by Yve Lepkowski (https://stolen-thyme.com/). Licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC-BY-SA 4.0). You may share and adapt this work with proper attribution.

attribution.name: Yve Lepkowski
attribution.note: Petit Lenormand — original deck, CC-BY-SA-4.0 (stolen-thyme.com/petit-lenormand); inspired by Joanna Newsom.

attribution.name: PlayfulProcess
attribution.role: compiled into this library as a recursive.eco grammar

_grammar_commons.modifications: Built the full 36-card set (the earlier import had 34 — restored 8 Cosmia and 9 Bouquet). 'The card' + 'Song reference' are her own text; 'Traditional meaning' is standard Lenormand, not hers. No meanings altered.

sections.The card: A person in a wide-brimmed hat rides a leaping horse along the seashore. In the background, a noose hangs from a scaffold near some small houses, and seagulls fly over the water.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Rider brings news, messages, and visitors. Something is approaching quickly. Expect communication or a new development arriving soon.
sections.Reversed: In Lenormand, reversal is not typically used. When negative: delayed news, unwanted visitor, or hasty decisions.
sections.Interpretation: The Rider brings news, messages, and visitors. Something is approaching quickly. Expect communication or a new development arriving soon.

sections.The card: A four-leafed clover standing in the grass, with a smaller three-leafed clover on either side. Underground, a horned dragon is curled up, surrounded by swirls which come from its mouth.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Clover represents small strokes of luck and fleeting opportunities. Not major fortune, but pleasant surprises and moments of good luck. Take the chance when it appears.
sections.Reversed: When negative: missed opportunity, luck running out, or gambling losses.
sections.Interpretation: The Clover represents small strokes of luck and fleeting opportunities. Not major fortune, but pleasant surprises and moments of good luck. Take the chance when it appears.

sections.The card: A four-masted ship floats on ocean waves. A birdcage hangs from its bowsprit, and its largest sail is decorated with a flying bird. Two hot air balloons rise in the sky behind the ship.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Ship represents travel, journeys, and foreign affairs. It can indicate physical travel, commerce, or something coming from afar. Movement toward distant goals.
sections.Reversed: When negative: cancelled travel, shipping delays, or feeling adrift.
sections.Interpretation: The Ship represents travel, journeys, and foreign affairs. It can indicate physical travel, commerce, or something coming from afar. Movement toward distant goals.

sections.The card: A one-story house with a chimney, in a field of poppies. Pine trees and mountains in the background cradle the house.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Home represents your dwelling, family, security, and comfort. It speaks to domestic matters, property, and the foundation of your life.
sections.Reversed: When negative: domestic problems, insecurity, or issues with property.
sections.Interpretation: The Home represents your dwelling, family, security, and comfort. It speaks to domestic matters, property, and the foundation of your life.

sections.The card: A rabbit crouches next to a small birch tree with falling leaves.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Tree represents health, slow growth, and deep roots. Things take time like a tree growing. It can indicate physical health or spiritual/karmic matters.
sections.Reversed: When negative: health issues, stagnation, or problems with long-term matters.
sections.Interpretation: The Tree represents health, slow growth, and deep roots. Things take time like a tree growing. It can indicate physical health or spiritual/karmic matters.

sections.The card: The crenellated towers and walls of a castle rise through swirling clouds. There are some scrubby grasses in the foreground.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Clouds represent confusion, uncertainty, and things not being clear. Like weather, this state is temporary. The dark side brings more confusion than the light side.
sections.Reversed: When negative: deep confusion, depression, or lies being told.
sections.Interpretation: The Clouds represent confusion, uncertainty, and things not being clear. Like weather, this state is temporary. The dark side brings more confusion than the light side.

items.name: Serpent (Snake)

sections.The card: A coiled serpent with fangs and a spade-shaped tail, surrounded by flames.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Serpent represents complications, detours, and sometimes a rival or enemy (often female). The path is not straight—expect twists and turns. Can also represent wisdom through experience.
sections.Reversed: When negative: betrayal, deception, or a dangerous person nearby.
sections.Interpretation: The Serpent represents complications, detours, and sometimes a rival or enemy (often female). The path is not straight—expect twists and turns. Can also represent wisdom through experience.

questions: Who might be working against me?

items.name: Scythe

sections.The card: A scythe rests on a pile of hay. Five black birds circle overhead.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Scythe cuts suddenly and decisively. It can represent sudden endings, accidents, surgery, or harvesting what you've sown. Cuts both good and bad ties.
sections.Reversed: When negative: accidents, painful endings, or surgical procedures.
sections.Interpretation: The Scythe cuts suddenly and decisively. It can represent sudden endings, accidents, surgery, or harvesting what you've sown. Cuts both good and bad ties.

questions: What needs to be cut away?

sections.The card: A woman brandishes a whip between two lines of hooded monks. A spider hangs from a large bow on the back of the woman’s dress.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Whip represents arguments, conflict, and repetitive actions. It can indicate sports, exercise, or passionate encounters. Something happens again and again.
sections.Reversed: When negative: abuse, fighting, or destructive patterns.
sections.Interpretation: The Whip represents arguments, conflict, and repetitive actions. It can indicate sports, exercise, or passionate encounters. Something happens again and again.

items.name: Birds

sections.The card: Two nightjars with tiny beaks and big eyes sit on branches.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Birds represent communication, conversations, and often gossip. They can indicate a couple (elderly or business partners) or nervous, chattering energy.
sections.Reversed: When negative: gossip, anxiety, or worrisome news.
sections.Interpretation: The Birds represent communication, conversations, and often gossip. They can indicate a couple (elderly or business partners) or nervous, chattering energy.

questions: What couples or pairs are involved?

sections.The card: A swaddled baby encircled by two leafy stems. There is a kite shape below the baby and stems with two blackberries and heart-shaped leaves above the baby.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Child represents new beginnings, actual children, or something small and innocent. Can indicate inexperience or something in early stages needing care.
sections.Reversed: When negative: immaturity, naivety, or a difficult child.
sections.Interpretation: The Child represents new beginnings, actual children, or something small and innocent. Can indicate inexperience or something in early stages needing care.

sections.The card: A fox prances by the side of a fishpond, with a fish in its mouth. There are pine trees in the background and decorative flourishes in the air.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Fox represents cunning, self-interest, and often employment. It warns of deception but also encourages smart strategies. Can indicate job or work situations.
sections.Reversed: When negative: deception, trickery, or job loss.
sections.Interpretation: The Fox represents cunning, self-interest, and often employment. It warns of deception but also encourages smart strategies. Can indicate job or work situations.

sections.The card: A bear stands waist-deep in the ocean. She looks up at the “big dipper” constellation in the sky.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Bear represents strength, power, and authority figures—often a boss or mother. Can indicate finances, investments, and the need for strength.
sections.Reversed: When negative: overbearing authority, financial problems, or jealousy.
sections.Interpretation: The Bear represents strength, power, and authority figures—often a boss or mother. Can indicate finances, investments, and the need for strength.

sections.The card: The constellation Cassiopeia, which is made up of five stars in the shape of a W.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Stars bring hope, inspiration, and guidance. They light the way forward. Can indicate technology, the internet, or spiritual guidance. A very positive card.
sections.Reversed: When negative: false hopes or feeling lost without guidance.
sections.Interpretation: The Stars bring hope, inspiration, and guidance. They light the way forward. Can indicate technology, the internet, or spiritual guidance. A very positive card.

sections.The card: A flying Canada goose. There are decorative flourishes in the air around the goose.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Goose (traditionally Stork) represents changes and transitions—usually positive. Associated with moving, improvement, and sometimes pregnancy or birth of new projects.
sections.Reversed: When negative: unwanted changes or restlessness.
sections.Interpretation: The Goose (traditionally Stork) represents changes and transitions—usually positive. Associated with moving, improvement, and sometimes pregnancy or birth of new projects.

items.name: Dog

sections.The card: A dog playfully lifts her tail in the air with her front legs and head low to the ground. She has a bone in her mouth and holds a pinecone between her front paws. There are decorative flourishes in the air around the dog.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Dog represents a loyal friend, trust, and faithfulness. It can indicate a specific friend or the quality of loyalty in a situation. Reliable support.
sections.Reversed: When negative: false friend, disloyalty, or being too dependent.
sections.Interpretation: The Dog represents a loyal friend, trust, and faithfulness. It can indicate a specific friend or the quality of loyalty in a situation. Reliable support.

questions: Where can I find faithful support?

sections.The card: A crenellated tower with two pointed arched windows. The tower has two legs, in pointy-toed boots. It stands on an assortment of bones, including a human skull.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Tower represents authority, institutions (government, corporations, hospitals), and sometimes isolation. It can indicate ambition or the need for boundaries.
sections.Reversed: When negative: loneliness, imprisonment, or bureaucratic obstacles.
sections.Interpretation: The Tower represents authority, institutions (government, corporations, hospitals), and sometimes isolation. It can indicate ambition or the need for boundaries.

sections.The card: A horned dragon and a person sit on a blanket in a garden. The person wears a cloak decorated with a cross. Behind them, there are rows of flowers and a low wall covered in heart-shaped ivy leaves. There is an arched door in the middle of the wall.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Garden represents social gatherings, the public, and your reputation. It can indicate parties, networking, or how you're perceived by society.
sections.Reversed: When negative: social problems, bad reputation, or unwanted public attention.
sections.Interpretation: The Garden represents social gatherings, the public, and your reputation. It can indicate parties, networking, or how you're perceived by society.

sections.The card: A domed observatory with a telescope sits on the side of a mountain whose base is enveloping in swirling clouds. A crescent moon hangs in the sky on the left side of the mountain, the sun is on the other side.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Mountain represents obstacles, delays, and blockages. Something stands in your way. It can also represent a stubborn enemy or the need for patience and persistence.
sections.Reversed: When negative: insurmountable problems or a powerful enemy.
sections.Interpretation: The Mountain represents obstacles, delays, and blockages. Something stands in your way. It can also represent a stubborn enemy or the need for patience and persistence.

sections.The card: A road in the foreground splits in two, traveling over rolling hills. On the left side of the card the road leads toward a city skyline. On the right side of the card the road leads toward a barn and trees.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Roads represent choices, decisions, and multiple options. You're at a crossroads and must choose a direction. Can indicate important decisions or freedom of choice.
sections.Reversed: When negative: confusion, indecision, or choosing wrong.
sections.Interpretation: The Roads represent choices, decisions, and multiple options. You're at a crossroads and must choose a direction. Can indicate important decisions or freedom of choice.

items.name: Rabbits (Mice)

sections.The card: Two running rabbits with bows around their necks. Decorative flourishes surround the rabbits.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Rabbits (traditionally Mice) represent small losses, theft, and things being nibbled away. Stress and worry deplete resources. Watch for things slowly disappearing.
sections.Reversed: When negative: significant theft, health issues, or persistent anxiety.
sections.Interpretation: The Rabbits (traditionally Mice) represent small losses, theft, and things being nibbled away. Stress and worry deplete resources. Watch for things slowly disappearing.

questions: What small worries gnaw at me?

items.name: Heart

sections.The card: A white heart is in the center of a starry night sky. At the bottom of the picture, there are outlines of buildings and mountains, and a ladder leads up to the heart.
sections.Song reference: Song: “You Will Not Take My Heart Alive”
sections.Traditional meaning: The Heart represents love, romance, and matters of the heart. It brings emotional warmth, passion, and happiness. A very positive card for love questions.
sections.Reversed: When negative: heartbreak, unrequited love, or emotional pain.
sections.Interpretation: The Heart represents love, romance, and matters of the heart. It brings emotional warmth, passion, and happiness. A very positive card for love questions.

questions: Where is love in this situation?

sections.The card: A ring with a knotted string around its top. Below the ring, there is a small circle and two spiraling stems with leaves on their ends.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Ring represents commitment, contracts, and binding agreements. It can indicate marriage, business partnerships, or the completion of a cycle. What goes around comes around.
sections.Reversed: When negative: broken promises, divorce, or unfulfilled contracts.
sections.Interpretation: The Ring represents commitment, contracts, and binding agreements. It can indicate marriage, business partnerships, or the completion of a cycle. What goes around comes around.

items.name: Book

sections.The card: A book with the title “Right On” and an image of a sun on its cover.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Book represents secrets, hidden knowledge, and education. Something is unknown or being concealed. Can indicate studies, research, or discovering hidden information.
sections.Reversed: When negative: dark secrets, ignorance, or hidden enemies.
sections.Interpretation: The Book represents secrets, hidden knowledge, and education. Something is unknown or being concealed. Can indicate studies, research, or discovering hidden information.

questions: What do I need to learn?

sections.The card: A person sits at a desk in the dark, writing on a scroll with a feather pen. The scroll is surrounded by a many-pointed star shape. There is a pointed arched window in the wall which looks out on a crescent moon and stars.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Letter represents documents, written communication, and official messages. Expect news in writing—emails, texts, contracts, or certificates.
sections.Reversed: When negative: bad news, legal problems, or delayed correspondence.
sections.Interpretation: The Letter represents documents, written communication, and official messages. Expect news in writing—emails, texts, contracts, or certificates.

items.name: Man (Gentleman)

sections.The card: A man wearing a hooded jacket and large boots stands in front of a mill wheel in  a creek, with his thumb hooked in his belt.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Man is a significator representing a significant male person in the reading—the querent if male, a partner, father, son, or important man in the situation.
sections.Reversed: When negative: a problematic man or masculine energy causing issues.
sections.Interpretation: The Man is a significator representing a significant male person in the reading—the querent if male, a partner, father, son, or important man in the situation.

questions: How is the male energy in this?

items.name: Lady (Woman)

sections.The card: A woman wearing a hooded jacket, large boots, and a kerchief around her hair stands with a basket near a washing line, where a dress hangs.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Lady is a significator representing a significant female person in the reading—the querent if female, a partner, mother, daughter, or important woman in the situation.
sections.Reversed: When negative: a problematic woman or feminine energy causing issues.
sections.Interpretation: The Lady is a significator representing a significant female person in the reading—the querent if female, a partner, mother, daughter, or important woman in the situation.

questions: How is the feminine energy in this?

sections.The card: A stuffed dove with a heart-shaped patch sewn on its chest. Its wing is made of a glove, the fingers of the glove forming the feathers.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Dove (traditionally Lily) represents peace, purity, and harmony. It can indicate mature wisdom, peaceful resolution, or in some traditions, sexuality and sensuality.
sections.Reversed: When negative: lack of peace, impurity, or elderly person's problems.
sections.Interpretation: The Dove (traditionally Lily) represents peace, purity, and harmony. It can indicate mature wisdom, peaceful resolution, or in some traditions, sexuality and sensuality.

sections.The card: The sun has both triangular and wavy rays. Below the sun, a black kettle decorated with a heart sits on a round cloth.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Sun is one of the most positive cards—success, happiness, energy, and warmth. It illuminates whatever it touches with positive energy. Victory and achievement.
sections.Reversed: When negative: burnout, arrogance, or temporary setbacks in success.
sections.Interpretation: The Sun is one of the most positive cards—success, happiness, energy, and warmth. It illuminates whatever it touches with positive energy. Victory and achievement.

sections.The card: Water flows from a crescent moon into a thimble.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Moon represents recognition, fame, and emotional matters. It highlights deep feelings, intuition, and how others see you. Associated with creative and romantic pursuits.
sections.Reversed: When negative: emotional confusion, depression, or damaged reputation.
sections.Interpretation: The Moon represents recognition, fame, and emotional matters. It highlights deep feelings, intuition, and how others see you. Associated with creative and romantic pursuits.

items.name: Key

sections.The card: A key with a heart drawn on its head is in the air over an open trunk. The trunk contains a high-heeled boot, three buttons, and a folded dress.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Key represents solutions, answers, and things of importance. It unlocks doors and indicates fate or destiny. Whatever it touches becomes significant or finds resolution.
sections.Reversed: When negative: locked doors, lost keys, or missing solutions.
sections.Interpretation: The Key represents solutions, answers, and things of importance. It unlocks doors and indicates fate or destiny. Whatever it touches becomes significant or finds resolution.

questions: What is the key to this situation?

sections.The card: A narwhal with a long horn. The narwhal is surrounded by decorative flourishes.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Fish represent money, business, and material abundance. They indicate financial matters, independence, and the flow of resources. A positive card for material concerns.
sections.Reversed: When negative: financial loss, greed, or problematic business dealings.
sections.Interpretation: The Fish represent money, business, and material abundance. They indicate financial matters, independence, and the flow of resources. A positive card for material concerns.

sections.The card: A person with long hair swims down toward an anchor surrounded by oysters. One oyster shell is open to reveal a pearl.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Anchor represents stability, work, and reaching your destination. It indicates perseverance paying off, job security, and finding safe harbor after a journey.
sections.Reversed: When negative: stuck, can't move forward, or unstable work situation.
sections.Interpretation: The Anchor represents stability, work, and reaching your destination. It indicates perseverance paying off, job security, and finding safe harbor after a journey.

sections.The card: A person’s ankles are wrapped in bandages. The ends of the two bandages trail down and cross each other, forming a X-shape.
sections.Traditional meaning: The Roads represent choices, decisions, and multiple options. You're at a crossroads and must choose a direction. Can indicate important decisions or freedom of choice.
sections.Reversed: When negative: heavy suffering, feeling cursed, or overwhelming burdens.
sections.Interpretation: The Cross represents burdens, fate, and karmic lessons. It can indicate suffering that must be endured or religious/spiritual matters. Heavy responsibility that shapes your path.

sections.The card: A frog in fancy clothing holds a bouquet of daisies.
A frog in fancy clothing holds a bouquet of daisies.
